page.content: In a study involving 33 participants in both the TOE Group and TOC Group, the time to achieve intubation was analyzed, revealing a T1 of 3.7 ± 1.6 seconds for the TOE Group versus 3.4 ± 0.8 seconds for the TOC Group, with a P-value of 0.278. Notably, the T2 times were significantly different, showing 34.9 ± 3.0 seconds for the TOE Group and 27.2 ± 2.5 seconds for the TOC Group, with a P-value of <0.0001. The total time to intubation was also markedly different, with the TOE Group at 38.7 ± 3.3 seconds and the TOC Group at 30.6 ± 2.7 seconds, both yielding a P-value of <0.0001. The number of intubation attempts was consistent at 33/0/0 for both groups, and neither group employed the BURP maneuver (0/33). The duration of tracheal operation times was 189.7 ± 92.3 minutes for the TOE Group and 184.8 ± 81.5 minutes for the TOC Group, resulting in a P-value of 0.822.

page.content: Additionally, concerning the antibody titre of the queen, seroconversion of kittens showed 4 with <1000 titre and 25% after the first vaccination, while 0 with 1000–<10000 and 0%, and 2* with >10000 and 9%. After the second vaccination, 9 kittens showed seroconversion with <1000 titre and 56%, 5 with 1000–<10000 and 36%, and none with >10000. Following the third vaccination, 1 kitten had <1000 titre (6%), 6 had 1000–<10000 (43%), and 4 had >10000 (17%). In total, seroconversion was recorded as 16 for <1000, 14 for 1000–<10000, and 23 for >10000 titre. The no seroconversion rates were 2 (13%), 3 (21%), and 17 (74%) across the respective titres.
